occurrence_sentence: Five French quantum computer-research startups were awarded contracts to develop two universal quantum computer prototypes by 2032.
France's defense-procurement agency selected Alice & Bob, C12, Pasqal, Quandela and Quobly for the project.
France's defense-procurement agency selected Alice & Bob, C12, Pasqal, Quandela and Quobly for the project.
The agency will invest up to €500 million (about US$545 million) in the project.

occurrence_sentence: France's defense-procurement agency announced the [awarding of contracts to five French computer-research startups](https://www.c4isrnet.com/battlefield-tech/2024/03/07/french-defense-ministry-picks-startups-to-develop-quantum-computers/), aimed at developing technology to create two universal quantum computer prototypes by 2032, according to [C4ISNET](https://www.c4isrnet.com/about-us/#:~:text=C4ISRNET%20is%20a%20digital%20brand,the%20network%2Dbased%20defense%20community.).

interaction_phrase: will oversee the program to develop quantum computer prototypes
occurrence_sentence: The Defense Digital Agency (AND), which operates under the procurement agency, will oversee the program to develop quantum computer prototypes.

interaction_phrase: build a hybrid quantum computing network designed to bridge traditional computing systems with quantum computers, thereby facilitating access for research institutions, startups, and industry partners
occurrence_sentence: This initiative builds on France's announcement two years ago that the nation would build a hybrid quantum computing network designed to bridge traditional computing systems with quantum computers, thereby facilitating access for research institutions, startups, and industry partners.
Four years into the program, the three most promising projects will be selected to advance the development of scalable logic qubits, C4ISNET reports.
This structured approach reflects France's commitment to leading in the quantum computing domain, potentially reshaping its defense landscape in the coming decades.
